channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — More than a week into what could be the costliest wildfires in U.S. history, weary and anxious Los Angeles-area residents were hoping for a favorable shift in the winds expected Thursday and an end to the constant fear that their lives might be further upended.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Florida grandmother who left infant in hot car found not guilty of manslaughter
item.description: A Florida grandmother was acquitted of manslaughter in the death of her 7-month-old granddaughter, but was convicted on a lesser charge.
item.dc:creator: Staci DaSilva, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bondi says she won't play politics as attorney general but doesn't rule out probes of Trump foes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pam Bondi, Donald Trump's pick for attorney general, sought to reassure Democratic senators Wednesday that her Justice Department would not prosecute anyone for political purposes but also refused to rule out the potential for investigations into adversaries of the Republican presi
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Southwest pilot arrested for suspected DUI at Georgia airport
item.description: A Southwest flight was delayed for five hours Wednesday morning due to a pilot being arrested and charged with DUI, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Palisades Fire firefighters taking part in first-of-its-kind cancer study
item.description: The study comes after the International Agency for Research on Cancer officially classified firefighting as a carcinogenic profession.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Smith, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US must not become complacent to a growing terrorism threat, a Counterterrorism Center official says
item.description: McLEAN, Va. (AP) — Brett Holmgren got woken up early on New Year's Day by alerts that a driver had plowed into a crowd of revelers in New Orleans. The rampage, which killed 14 people, was the deadliest attack on U.S. soil in years and was inspired by the Islamic State group.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FAFSA forms to make October release after two-year struggle
item.description: The Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) forms will be released on Oct. 1 this year, the Department of Education announced on Wednesday, hitting the normal deadline after two chaotic years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Family attorney of fraternity pledge left debilitated from hazing cheers new law as good start but not enough
item.description: Mizzou student Danny Santulli was left blind and unable to walk after a hazing ritual in 2021. His family is now cheering the passage of a new anti-hazing law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Live coverage: Biden's farewell speech comes on heels of raucous confirmation hearings
item.description: President Biden delivered a somewhat grim primetime farewell speech on Wednesday from the Oval Office to mark the final days of his presidency.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, WGN 9

item.title: Man in black Jeep wanted in California after stalking girls leaving school, police say
item.description: A man driving a black Jeep is being sought in Northern California after authorities say he stalked a group of girls leaving school on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More of Trump's dream team heads to the Hill and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Machete suspect nabbed before Trump Capitol visit allegedly ranted about president-elect online
item.description: Anti-Trump posts discovered on account linked to Capitol machete suspect who tried to enter with knives while President Jimmy Carter was lying in state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Windy, flame-fanning weather eases up as progress made on LA-area fires
item.description: Windy, flame-fanning weather in Southern California has eased up as firefighters report significant gains against the two massive wildfires burning around Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Jaimie Ding And Julie Watson And John Seewer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fire tornadoes pose a threat in California. A fire lab shows how they work
item.description: The National Weather Service's warning about the “particularly dangerous situation” in which any new fire could explode in size in the Los Angeles area didn’t mention fire tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: Holly Ramer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AAPI adults prioritize immigration, but split on mass deportations: AP-NORC/AAPI Data poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Immigration is an issue that many Asian Americans, Native Hawaiians and Pacific Islanders want the government to focus on this year, but they are sharply divided on what actions President-elect Donald Trump should take when he returns to the White House, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dire fire warning for LA area pushed back as winds ease
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Easing winds delivered a brief but much-needed reprieve to firefighters Tuesday as they battled two massive blazes burning in the Los Angeles area, and the National Weather Service pushed back its unusually dire warning of critical fire weather until early the following day.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A retro blue VW van miraculously survives deadly Los Angeles fire
item.description: Preston Martin figured the retro blue Volkswagen van he slept in for a year during college was a goner, given that he parked it in a Malibu neighborhood just before the Palisades fire ripped through, reducing homes and cars to rubble and charred metal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: Girls basketball coach accused of hitting person with car after argument
item.description: A girls' youth basketball coach is accused of hitting a person with his vehicle after an argument following a game Saturday in Columbia, according to state police, and it was caught on surveillance video.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, WGN 9

item.title: Prominent Puerto Rican civil rights leader José 'Cha Cha' Jiménez dies at 76
item.description: José “Cha Cha” Jiménez, a prominent civil rights and liberation movement figure and founder of the Young Lords in Chicago and co-founder of the Rainbow Coalition has died. He was 76. His sister, Daisy Rodríguez, said in a Facebook post that he died Friday morning. A cause of death was not given.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida bill would ban students in the US without legal permission from some public universities
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida state lawmaker has filed a bill that would ban some public colleges and universities from admitting immigrants who are in the country without legal permission. The proposal comes the day after Gov.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Capital One sued by US watchdog, accused of cheating customers out of $2 billion
item.description: A U.S. watchdog is suing Capital One for allegedly misleading consumers about its offerings for high-interest savings accounts — and “cheating" customers out of more than $2 billion in lost interest payments as a result.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: REAL ID enforcement may be phased, but deadline is the same: What to know
item.description: The REAL ID deadline is fast-approaching and officials want you to be prepared (spoiler: you may already be in compliance).
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Interactive maps show homes damaged, destroyed in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The Los Angeles County Fire Department has provided maps of the thousands of structures, primarily homes, that were damaged or destroyed in the Palisades and Eaton wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama is skipping President-elect Trump's inauguration next week, after raising eyebrows by not attending former President Jimmy Carter's funeral in Washington, D.C., last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Princess Kate says her cancer is in remission
item.description: The Princess of Wales revealed Tuesday that her cancer is in remission after an emotional visit to the hospital where she received treatment last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Rashida Jones is stepping down as MSNBC president on the eve of Trump inauguration
item.description: MSNBC President Rashida Jones says she is stepping down after four years leading the liberal news network, her move coming on the eve of a second Trump administration and after changes in corporate ownership.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: READ: Special counsel's final report in Trump Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department released special counsel Jack Smith's report on President-elect Donald Trump's election interference case, just days before he returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, WGN 9

item.title: Poll reveals how much voters blame climate change, Newsom for California fire crisis
item.description: In the wake of several deadly California wildfires a new poll shows how Americans are reacting to the disaster, which left at least two dozen people dead and reduced entire neighborhoods to charred rubble.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Democrats' hopes of derailing Trump nominees fading fast
item.description: Democrats' hopes of defeating any of President-elect Trump's Cabinet nominees appear to be fizzling as Senate committees prepare for the first week of hearings.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: What to make of the final polling of the Biden years
item.description: A January 10-11 survey of 1,000 registered voters found President Joe Biden with a 37% approval rating in the final week of his presidency, 12 points lower than when Emerson first polled his approval in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles price gouging is 'inhumane': Real estate agent
item.description: Nikko Santo Pietro, Los Angeles real estate agent and son of 'Wheel of Fortune’s' Vanna White, said the supply of housing is “so limited.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, Fox 5

item.title: Abbott: Flags will be at full staff outside Texas buildings on Inauguration Day
item.description: Gov. Gregg Abbott announced on Monday he will order flags outside all state buildings to be at half staff on Jan. 20 in honor of Inauguration Day, according to a news release from the governor's office.
item.dc:creator: Erica Pauda, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Traffic into Manhattan drops 7.5% in first week of new toll. That's 43,000 fewer cars a day
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A new toll on drivers entering the core of Manhattan brought modest but measurable traffic reductions to New York City’s heavily-gridlocked streets in its first week of operation, according to preliminary data released Monday by the state's transit authority.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Heidi Montag tops iTunes chart after losing house in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: Reality television star Heidi Montag's 2010 album “Superficial” has hit No. 1 on iTunes' all-genre songs and albums charts — 15 years after its release and just after Montag and husband Spencer Pratt lost their home in the Palisades Fire.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Photos: Handful of L.A. homes remain untouched amid charred neighborhoods
item.description: The Walsh House, featured in the “Beverly Hills 90210" television series, survived. So did its pristine lawn. Next door, the remains of a neighbor's home was blackened with soot.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oscars push back nominations announcement amid Calif. wildfires
item.description: With fires still active in the Los Angeles area, the film academy also extended the nominations voting period for its members through Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Judge clears the way for release of special counsel Smith's report on Trump's Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department can publicly release its investigative report on President-elect Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case, a federal judge said Monday — the latest ruling in a court dispute over the highly anticipated document days before Trump is set to take office again.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
